mission.title: Install and Configure Kyverno (Policy Engine)
mission.description: Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that allows users to manage security, compliance, and governance through policy-as-code. It is particularly useful for teams looking to enforce policies without needing to learn a new language. Key trade-offs include the need for careful policy management to avoid unintended consequences.

steps.title: Add the Kyverno Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kyverno Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Kyverno repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kyverno
steps.description:
This step installs Kyverno using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kyverno

helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version v1.17.1

```

> Create a namespace for Kyverno.
> Installs Kyverno in the specified namespace.
commands: helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version v1.17.1

steps.title: Verify Kyverno installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kyverno pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Lists all pods in the kyverno namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create a sample policy
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Kyverno policy to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

```

> Creates a policy that requires Pods to have an 'app' label.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

steps.title: Verify the policy
steps.description:
This step checks if the policy is enforced correctly.

```bash

kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --restart=Never

kubectl get events

```

> Attempt to create a Pod without the required label.
> Check for events indicating policy enforcement.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kyverno
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kyverno and all associated resources. Be aware that any policies created will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno

kubectl delete namespace kyverno

```

> Uninstalls the Kyverno release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kyverno
upgrade.description:
Backup existing policies, upgrade Kyverno, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get clusterpolicies -o yaml > backup-policies.yaml

helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version v1.17.1

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Backup existing policies to a file.
> Upgrades Kyverno to the specified version.
> Verify that the Kyverno pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version v1.17.1

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a policy is preventing the resource from being created.

**Fix:**
Check the policy rules and ensure the resource meets the required specifications.

troubleshooting.title: Kyverno pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen due to misconfiguration in the policies or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kyverno' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Policy not enforcing as expected
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The policy may not be applied correctly or might be in a 'dry-run' mode.

**Fix:**
Ensure the policy is set to 'enforce' and applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to the Kyverno service
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and any network policies in place.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new validation rules and improved error messages.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kyverno pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kyverno'.

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
